blocks.content: The faculty office was unusually busy.
Most teachers would chat or drink tea between classes, but now they were all sorting through paperwork.
Classifying dozens of club applications, recording them in reports, and forwarding them to the responsible departments was more labor-intensive than expected.
Siena, who'd just finished teaching, joined the effort without complaint.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: My, all these applications? They're pouring in this week.

blocks.speaker: Teacher
blocks.content: Hoho. It's the end of the semester. This always happens.
With the semester ending and vacation starting, students had to submit free-topic papers by the next term.
But these projects could be replaced by club presentations, so even unaffiliated students tended to join clubs temporarily around this time.

blocks.speaker: Sad
blocks.content: Kids these days are all about cutting corners. It wasn't like this before. Clubs and projects used to be about passion. Nowadays, they're too focused on efficiency.

blocks.speaker: Teacher
blocks.content: Haha! Times have changed. Competition is fiercer than ten years ago, and the kids are under more stress. Forming temporary clubs buys them time for personal study.

blocks.speaker: Sad
blocks.content: That's true, but aren't they too fixated on results? School should have some romance too.
The teacher could only offer a bitter smile in agreement.
As he prepared to forward the documents, Siena noticed one had fallen under the desk.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Here's a missing one.

blocks.speaker: Teacher
blocks.content: Huh? When did this come in? Must've been left during class.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Fufu. Someone must've been in a hurry.
Curious about which student had tried to cut corners, Siena examined the form.
Upon seeing the name, she adjusted her glasses in disbelief.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: This says… Shirone, right?

blocks.speaker: Teacher
blocks.content: Indeed. Arian Shirone. I thought he wasn't interested in clubs. Well, with everyone gaming the system, it's understandable.
Sad snatched the form and frowned.

blocks.speaker: Sad
blocks.content: That doesn't seem right. This club—it's quite infamous, isn't it?
Siena took the form back and checked the club Shirone had joined.
It was indeed infamous.
The name struck like lightning in a clear sky.
Recalling the annual battles with graduates from this club made her head throb.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Wait… Now that I think about it…
Hadn't most of the troublemakers from the Supernatural Parapsychology Club graduated last year?
She vaguely remembered hearing that funding had been cut due to a lack of members.

blocks.speaker: Teacher
blocks.content: Come to think of it, Iruki's also part of this club. Maybe they get along.

blocks.speaker: Sad
blocks.content: I heard they were impressive during the targeting test. But I wouldn't have guessed they'd be close. They don't seem like a good match.
Siena massaged her temples.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Yes. That's the problem.
If the genius Iruki had persuaded him, even the stubborn Shirone might've been swayed.
But Iruki?
The school's biggest troublemaker. Though both were geniuses, Shirone and Iruki pursued opposite paths.
They might help each other grow—or one might consume the other, leading to mutual destruction.
The school would suffer a huge loss either way.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Maybe it's for the best. Let's sever this cursed chain here.
Siena flipped through the documents to check who the club president was.
As expected, it was Neid.
The two closest to Shirone lately were both in this club.
Now she understood why Shirone, who'd avoided clubs until now, had joined.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Neid's homeroom teacher is…
Siena scanned the teachers' names to find the responsible faculty member.
Upon seeing it, she sighed and rubbed her forehead.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Sigh. Of all people…
On the outskirts of the magic academy stood a training hall.
Etela sat cross-legged in meditation.
The Carthis Monastery, where she belonged, was the kingdom's largest, with over a thousand monk trainees.
Hundreds of children entered each year, but only a handful were recognized as priests.
Etela stood out even among them.
She'd surpassed high-ranking nobles—even royalty—to become the youngest bishop.
The title of bishop wasn't earned by skill alone.
Etela had caught the elders' eyes because of her innate saintly nature.
To become the light and salt of the world.
With this vow, Etela had turned her back on wealth and fame to become a teacher at Alpheas Magic Academy.

blocks.speaker: Etela
blocks.content: Thank you. For allowing me to safely complete another day of education.
Thus, Etela differed from other teachers.
Her background wasn't a school or magic association but the monastery.
If mages viewed the world critically, monks sought to embrace it whole without analysis.
Infinite gratitude to nature for her existence, and a sense of duty to share that feeling with others—that was all Etela was.
She slowly opened her eyes.
Her irises shimmered, piercing the void.
Through the Spirit Zone, she sensed a woman approaching.
Ordinary Spirit Zones couldn't discern gender, but Etela was a Joiner—a master of the Spirit Zone—and her detection skills surpassed most mages.
If a normal Spirit Zone was like air, a Joiner's was like liquid.
The denser medium allowed for far clearer sensory perception.
Etela rose and waited.
After a long while, Siena entered, and Etela greeted her with a smile.

blocks.speaker: Etela
blocks.content: Welcome, Teacher Siena.
Siena wasn't surprised Etela had been expecting her.
She knew how advanced a Joiner's Spirit Zone was.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: You were meditating. Am I interrupting?

blocks.speaker: Etela
blocks.content: Not at all. I'd just finished. Please, come in.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Then pardon my intrusion.
Though kneeling on a cushion was unfamiliar, Siena sat respectfully and accepted the tea Etela offered.
Following monastery etiquette, she remained silent until finishing the bitter tea.
Though summer days were long, the golden sunset had already deepened.
Wanting to start before dark, Siena emptied her cup quickly.

blocks.speaker: Etela
blocks.content: You wouldn't come all this way for something trivial.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Yes. There's something I'd like to discuss.

blocks.speaker: Etela
blocks.content: You're Neid's homeroom teacher, correct?

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Yes. He's bright and kind. His grades are above average, and he's talented. He gets along well with others too.

blocks.speaker: Etela
blocks.content: He's the president of the Supernatural Parapsychology Club, right?

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Of course. Did Neid do something wrong?

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Yes. I plan to disband the club he leads. As his teacher, I wanted to inform you first.

blocks.speaker: Etela
blocks.content: If it's your decision, it must be for the students' sake. But this time, I'd like to hear your reasons.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: The Supernatural Parapsychology Club has always been problematic. It was notorious even before I arrived.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Of course, its achievements matched, so the school tolerated it. But things have changed.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: According to records, it hasn't held a single official presentation in three years. It's done nothing but cause trouble deserving of punishment.

blocks.speaker: Etela
blocks.content: But as you said, that's in the past, isn't it? The current members are Neid, Iruki, and Shirone. Class Four-caliber students. Shouldn't we see what they do?

blocks.speaker: Etela
blocks.content: Besides, the Supernatural Parapsychology Club is one of Alpheas's oldest. Guildmaster Gauld was a member, wasn't he?
